_meta.principle: Major DHH labels with verified rosters. Cross-checked against label sites + Wikipedia.

labels.name: Gully Gang
labels.blurb: The Mumbai gully wave's home base. Founded by Divine off the back of Mere Gully Mein. Roster reads like a Dharavi-to-Andheri map.

labels.name: Azadi Records
labels.blurb: The conscious wave's institutional spine. Prabh Deep's Class-Sikh put them on the map. Seedhe Maut left for DL91 in 2023. Still home base for political DHH.

labels.name: DL91 (Era)
labels.blurb: Independence move. After NAYAAB and BAYAAN broke the duo out, they split from Azadi and built DL91 to put out Lunch Break and beyond on their own terms.

labels.name: Mass Appeal India
labels.founder: Nas + Universal Music India joint venture
labels.blurb: The major-label gateway. Brought Talwiinder, Yelhomie. Hanumankind operated adjacent. The label that lets DHH cross over without losing the gully.

labels.name: Kalamkaar
labels.blurb: Raftaar's home for the pen-game roster. KR$NA's catalogue lives here; Karma and Ikka pass through.

labels.name: Bantai Records
labels.blurb: Emiway's empire. Built off Machayenge. Shez, Young Galib graduated from here. The independent answer to the major labels.

labels.name: Def Jam India
labels.blurb: The American import with Indian roots. Raga, Dino James, Fotty Seven are flagship. Hanumankind's global breakout went through their parent network.

labels.name: 5911 Records
labels.blurb: Sidhu's independent label. So High, 295, The Last Ride all came through it. The blueprint for Punjabi rap independence.

labels.name: Run-Up Records
labels.blurb: The diaspora label. AP Dhillon + Gurinder Gill built it for Brown Munde and never looked back. Republic Records distribution makes them stadium-ready.

labels.name: Warner Music India / 91 North
labels.blurb: The Punjabi crossover engine. Karan Aujla, Channi Nattan, King all sit under variants of the Warner umbrella in India.
